chunks.text: Stereo. H C J D A 38 Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, LAHORE (JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT) Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 Qamar Abbas versus Muhammad Akbar & 15-others Date of hearing 27.03.2019 The Appellant by M/s Ch. Muhammad Anwar Bhindar, Faisal Saghir Rana, Advocates The Respondents by Mr. Muhammad Arshad (In person) Gondal, Advocate The State by Mr. Munir Ahmad Sial, Deputy Prosecutor General ======= Asjad Javaid Ghural, J. Through this appeal under Section 25 (4-A) of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 appellant Qamar Abbas has challenged the vires of judgment dated 22.05.2008 passed by the learned Judge, Anti-Terrorism Court-I, Gujranwala in case FIR No.157 dated 16.04.2006, in respect of offences under Sections 302, 324, 449, 109, 148 & 149 PPC and Section 7(a), 7(c) & 21(I) of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 registered at Police Station, Dinga, District Gujrat whereby respondents No.1 to 15 (hereinafter called the respondents) were acquitted of the charge. 2. The crime report (Ex.PG) was registered on the complaint of appellant Qamar Abbas on 16.04.2006 wherein he alleged that on the same day at 8:30 p.m., he alongwith his maternal grand-father Muhammad Wilayat, paternal cousin (Phoophizad) Saifullah, his parents and siblings, was present in the house when Muhammad Aslam (since dead), Irshad (since PO) both armed with Kalashnikovs, Ghazi pistol 30-bore, Muhammad Zaman with 444-bore, Zafar Iqbal son of Bahadar, Muhammad Akbar and Wajahat respective rifles 8-MM, Shujaat 7-MM, Zulfiqar gun-12 bore, Ibrar alias Mithu Kalashnikov, Afzaal and Anar, guns 12-bore, Iftikhar 444-bore, Sajid Kalanshikov, Maalik 8-MM, Amjad 7-MM, Bashir 444-bore, Mehndi gun 12-bore, Zafar son of Shafi pistol 30-bore alongwith 5/6 unknown co-accused, armed with fire arm weapons, trespassed into the house while raising Lalkaras and caused fire arm injuries to his parents Umar Hayat and Mst.

Through this appeal under Section 25 (4-A) of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 appellant Qamar Abbas has challenged the vires of judgment dated 22.05.2008 passed by the learned Judge, Anti-Terrorism Court-I, Gujranwala in case FIR No.157 dated 16.04.2006, in respect of offences under Sections 302, 324, 449, 109, 148 & 149 PPC and Section 7(a), 7(c) & 21(I) of the Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997 registered at Police Station, Dinga, District Gujrat whereby respondents No.1 to 15 (hereinafter called the respondents) were acquitted of the charge. 2. The crime report (Ex.PG) was registered on the complaint of appellant Qamar Abbas on 16.04.2006 wherein he alleged that on the same day at 8:30 p.m., he alongwith his maternal grand-father Muhammad Wilayat, paternal cousin (Phoophizad) Saifullah, his parents and siblings, was present in the house when Muhammad Aslam (since dead), Irshad (since PO) both armed with Kalashnikovs, Ghazi pistol 30-bore, Muhammad Zaman with 444-bore, Zafar Iqbal son of Bahadar, Muhammad Akbar and Wajahat respective rifles 8-MM, Shujaat 7-MM, Zulfiqar gun-12 bore, Ibrar alias Mithu Kalashnikov, Afzaal and Anar, guns 12-bore, Iftikhar 444-bore, Sajid Kalanshikov, Maalik 8-MM, Amjad 7-MM, Bashir 444-bore, Mehndi gun 12-bore, Zafar son of Shafi pistol 30-bore alongwith 5/6 unknown co-accused, armed with fire arm weapons, trespassed into the house while raising Lalkaras and caused fire arm injuries to his parents Umar Hayat and Mst. Shafqat Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 2 Naseem, siblings Ume Adeeba Noreen, Um-e-Sana, Um-ul-Rehman, Ali Hassan, Shahid Raza, Mst. Afshan Shaznam, Um-e-Farwa and Umar Farooq. Mst. Afshan Shaznam, Um-e-Farwa and Umar Farooq survived subsequently whereas the remaining seven injured succumbed to the injuries at the spot. Muhammad Wilayat and Saifullah had also witnessed the occurrence in the light of electricity. Motive behind the occurrence was that one year prior to this incident, one Muhammad Ayub, a brother of Muhammad Aslam was murdered and case regarding his murder was registered against the complainant party. 3. Muhammad Aslam, SI (PW-17) taken all necessary steps during investigation and after completion of investigation submitted challan for trial against the respondents. 4. The prosecution produced 18-witnesses, one Muhammad Afzal, SI was examined as Court Witness. The respondents, in their statements recorded under Section 342 Cr.P.C. had denied and controverted all the allegations leveled against them and also produced eleven witnesses in their defence. 5. Learned trial Court, upon conclusion of the trial, acquitted the respondents. Hence this criminal appeal against their acquittal. 6. Before dilating upon facts of the case, it would be fitness of things to mention here that at the end of trial, Muhammad Aslam, Allah Ditta, Muhammad Irshad, Muhammad Zaman, Iftikhar Ahmad, Wajaat, Bushra, Sumaira and Saima were declared proclaimed offenders. Out of the aforesaid proclaimed offenders, Iftihar Ahmad, Wajaat Hussain, Allah Ditta, Muhammad Zaman, Bushra, Sumaira and Saima were arrested, tried and ultimately acquitted of the charge vide judgment dated 11.08.2015.

Before dilating upon facts of the case, it would be fitness of things to mention here that at the end of trial, Muhammad Aslam, Allah Ditta, Muhammad Irshad, Muhammad Zaman, Iftikhar Ahmad, Wajaat, Bushra, Sumaira and Saima were declared proclaimed offenders. Out of the aforesaid proclaimed offenders, Iftihar Ahmad, Wajaat Hussain, Allah Ditta, Muhammad Zaman, Bushra, Sumaira and Saima were arrested, tried and ultimately acquitted of the charge vide judgment dated 11.08.2015. The appellant has preferred appeal against their acquittal (Criminal Appeal No.1671 of 2015), wherein the notices were issued to the acquitted respondents and today, the same is going to be decided through even dated separate judgment. Accused Muhammad Aslam and Muhammad Irshad remained proclaimed offenders and at present, it has been stated at bar that after pronouncement of judgments in both the aforesaid separate trials, Muhammad Aslam was arrested but he passed away in jail premises during trial whereas Muhammad Irshad is still at large. Co-accused Shujaat was Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 3 declared proclaimed offender during trial vide order dated 19.09.2006 whereas, a separate report under Section 173 Cr.P.C. was submitted to the extent of accused Muhammad Afzaal and he was tried separately under the Juvenile Justice System Ordinance, 2000. 7. The crime report was registered against 19-nominted and 5/6 unknown accused persons with the allegation to have trespassed into the house of the complainant by night, made indiscriminate firing, which resulted into the death of Umar Hayat, Mst. Shafqat Naseem, Ume Adeeba Noreen, Ume Sana, Um-ul-Rehman, Ali Hassan and Shahid Raza and fire arm injuries to Mst. Afshan Shaznam, Ume Farwa and Umar Farooq. During investigation, the complainant made statement before the police and disclosed the names of unknown accused as Asjad Mehmood, Muhammad Azam and Allah Ditta. 8. The nominated accused namely, Mst. Zahida, Mst. Nusrat, Mst. Anam, Mst. Bushra, Mst. Sumaira and Mst. Saima were alleged to have hatchet behind-the-scene conspiracy. Mst. Zahida and Mst. Nusrat Iqbal were tried earlier, Mst.

Zahida and Mst. Nusrat Iqbal were tried earlier, Mst. Anam, being juvenile, was tried separately and no appeal against her acquittal has been preferred by the complainant whereas, respondents Bushra, Sumaira and Saima were tried in the subsequent trial. In first trial, the prosecution examined Mst. Sajida Bibi (PW-13) and Muhammad Abbas (PW-14) to prove the charge of hatching conspiracy against the lady respondents stating that on 15.04.2006 at 10:00 a.m., they alongwith one Khalid (not produced) had heard the conversation of the lady respondents hatching conspiracy while sitting in their Kurh (cattle-pen) stating that the soul of Ayub would rest in peace if they take revenge in retaliation by killing the family of Umar Hayat. During cross-examination, the deposition of both the aforesaid witnesses was found contradictory to each other qua the time and place of hearing conversation of conspiracy. They did not disclose this fact well in time to the complainant party so that the measures could be adopted to avoid the incident even though they knew that it was the eve of death anniversary of Muhammad Ayub where the family members of the principal accused namely, Muhammad Aslam had gathered for condolence. The place where they heard the conversation of conspiracy was belonging to the accused party and there was no occasion for Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 4 both the aforesaid witnesses to hear their conversation despite blood-feud enmity with them and that too by six young ladies including one minor girl aged about 10-years (Anum). For safe administration of justice, we concur with the observation arrived at by the learned trial court qua the acquittal of the respondent ladies including the ladies tried in separate trial. 9.

Appeal No.601 of 2008 4 both the aforesaid witnesses to hear their conversation despite blood-feud enmity with them and that too by six young ladies including one minor girl aged about 10-years (Anum). For safe administration of justice, we concur with the observation arrived at by the learned trial court qua the acquittal of the respondent ladies including the ladies tried in separate trial. 9. Respondents Muhammad Azam, Asjad Mehmood and Allah Ditta (tried separately) were introduced by the complainant through supplementary statement being the unknown accused. Muhammad Azam was maternal uncle of respondent Sajid Iqbal (son-in-law of principal accused Muhammad Aslam), Asjad Mehmood was a friend of accused Afzaal (maternal nephew of Muhammad Aslam). In such backdrop, it has been established on record that they were already known to the complainant party. If they were accompanying the other nominated accused at the time of the incident, they would have definitely been named in the crime report and the question of their non-identification during trial stands vanished especially when the complainant himself admitted that he identified them at the spot. The subsequent development as to the identity of the aforesaid respondents appears to have been maneuvered in order to spread the net wide. The reasons given by learned trial court qua the acquittal of aforesaid respondent/accused were quite justified and need no interference by this court. 10. Now, we take up the case of nominated respondents i.e. Muhammad Akbar, Zafar Iqbal son of Bahadar, Sarfraz alias Ibrar alias Mithu, Sajid Iqbal, Muhammad Maalik, Zafar Iqbal son of Shafi, Amjad Iqbal, Bashir Ahmad, Mehndi Khan, Zulfiqar and Muhammad Anar (tried earlier), Iftikhar Ahmad, Wajahat Hussain and Muhammad Zaman (tried in subsequent trial). The summary of their acquittal prevailing with the learned trial court is that:- a) The statements of all the injured persons was recorded with the delay of 15-days without plausible explanation, b) Injured Mst.

Muhammad Akbar, Zafar Iqbal son of Bahadar, Sarfraz alias Ibrar alias Mithu, Sajid Iqbal, Muhammad Maalik, Zafar Iqbal son of Shafi, Amjad Iqbal, Bashir Ahmad, Mehndi Khan, Zulfiqar and Muhammad Anar (tried earlier), Iftikhar Ahmad, Wajahat Hussain and Muhammad Zaman (tried in subsequent trial). The summary of their acquittal prevailing with the learned trial court is that:- a) The statements of all the injured persons was recorded with the delay of 15-days without plausible explanation, b) Injured Mst. Afshan Bibi had hide herself behind the refrigerator at the time of launching murderous assault by the respondents then how she categorically nominated the accused with specific weapon. Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 5 c) Complainant Qamar Abbas claimed that the assailants were wearing police uniform when his sister Mst. Afshan Bibi opened the door, they trespassed into the house and made indiscriminate firing then how he alongwith his maternal grand-father Muhammad Walayat and cousin Saifullah managed to go into the kitchen and remained safe. d) The dead bodies of the deceased as well as injured witnesses were lying scattered in the courtyard and rooms at different distances, some at a distance of more than five feet but the medical evidence contradict it observing blackening around most of the injuries sustained by all the deceased as well as the injured witnesses. e) The prosecution withheld two material independent witnesses namely, Muhammad Walayat and Saifullah. f) During investigation conducted by different police officers up to the rank of Superintendent of Police, the respondents were found to be not involved in this occurrence and no incriminating material including the weapon of offence was recovered from them. g) It was night time occurrence and identification of the assailants with specific weapons of offence was a difficult task. h) The prosecution did not let even a single member of the opponent’s family including a child of 10-years of age (Maroof alias Ghazi) and a man of advanced age of 80-years (Mehandi). 11. We have deep concern qua the reasons of acquittal of the respondents by the learned trial court.

Afshan Bibi opened the door, they trespassed into the house and made indiscriminate firing then how he alongwith his maternal grand-father Muhammad Walayat and cousin Saifullah managed to go into the kitchen and remained safe. d) The dead bodies of the deceased as well as injured witnesses were lying scattered in the courtyard and rooms at different distances, some at a distance of more than five feet but the medical evidence contradict it observing blackening around most of the injuries sustained by all the deceased as well as the injured witnesses. e) The prosecution withheld two material independent witnesses namely, Muhammad Walayat and Saifullah. f) During investigation conducted by different police officers up to the rank of Superintendent of Police, the respondents were found to be not involved in this occurrence and no incriminating material including the weapon of offence was recovered from them. g) It was night time occurrence and identification of the assailants with specific weapons of offence was a difficult task. h) The prosecution did not let even a single member of the opponent’s family including a child of 10-years of age (Maroof alias Ghazi) and a man of advanced age of 80-years (Mehandi). 11. We have deep concern qua the reasons of acquittal of the respondents by the learned trial court. No doubt the statement of the injured girl was recorded with the delay of 15-days but the testimony of Muhammad Aslam, SI (PW-17) has much relevancy in that regard. He categorically deposed that on arrival at the spot, he dispatched the injured persons to the hospital without recording their statements under Section 161 Cr.P.C., on his application (Ex.PHHH), made on the very next day of the occurrence i.e. 17.04.2006 at 6:45 p.m., the medical officer opined that injured Mst. Afshan Bibi was fit to make statement but he did not record her statement. It is also evident from his deposition that he recorded the statement of the said injured girl later on without showing any plausible reason that why after getting permission from the doctor, he did not record her statement on the same day i.e. 17.04.2006. On the other hand, Mst. Afshan Bibi categorically deposed Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 6 that she made statement before the investigating officer on 17.04.2006 but when the said statement was not found available on the record, she expressed her ignorance whether it was reduced into writing by the police officer or not. It is crystal clear that the investigating officer was in league with the accused party, he misplaced or did not reduce into writing the statement of the injured lady made by her on 17.04.2006. The matter does not end here, the investigating officer did not utter even a single word that the injured persons had refused to make statement on the very day of the occurrence rather he himself deposed that he did not record the statement of Mst. Afshan despite getting permission from the doctor.

The matter does not end here, the investigating officer did not utter even a single word that the injured persons had refused to make statement on the very day of the occurrence rather he himself deposed that he did not record the statement of Mst. Afshan despite getting permission from the doctor. It should not be lost sight of the fact that Mst. Afshan Bibi had received five entry wounds through and through on different parts of her body including chest and in such situation when she remained admitted in the hospital for 15/20 days, the investigating officer was bound to record her statement but he negligently and due to biasness, did not perform his duty and the complainant party should not suffer. 12. True, that the complainant, his maternal grandfather Walayat Khan and a cousin Saifullah did not receive any injury during the process of making indiscriminate firing by 25-assailants but at the same time how should we ignore that seven persons had lost their lives and three others received injuries due to the act of making indiscriminate firing by the respondents. It is common and general as well that when such a situation emerges abruptly, the party aggressed upon prefers to hide in a manner whatever seems appropriate at that very moment. It has been highlighted that the aforesaid non-injured witnesses hide themselves in the kitchen having small entrance from the room where they were busy in conversation at that time, which was not unusual because, in their view, the respondents trespassed into the house, made indiscriminate firing at the inmates and after considering them dead, fled away but it was good-luck of three, out of ten, to survive. It was not essential for each and every assailant to search the house from four corners because they had already succeeded to kill almost whole family. We are surprised that at one stage learned trial court believed the presence of the eye witnesses in their own house i.e. the place of occurrence at the relevant time rather considered it as admitted one but at the same

It was not essential for each and every assailant to search the house from four corners because they had already succeeded to kill almost whole family. We are surprised that at one stage learned trial court believed the presence of the eye witnesses in their own house i.e. the place of occurrence at the relevant time rather considered it as admitted one but at the same Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 7 breath, questioned their truthfulness merely on the score that they did not receive any injury at the spot. We are not inclined to concur with this self-contradictory view. 13. Learned trial court observed that the dead bodies of the deceased as well as injured witnesses were lying scattered in the courtyard and rooms at different places and distances, some at a distance of more than five feet but the medical evidence contradict it observing blackening around most of the injuries sustained by all the deceased as well as the injured witnesses. It has straightaway been observed that the prosecution did not explain the distance between the injured and the assailants rather it was alleged that the respondents kept on making firing on the person whosoever they found without making any distinction. Some of the injured had received injuries with close range while some in running position. Some of the respondents entered into the room and returned after completion of task, then they all fled away. The factum of presence of blackening on some of the wounds received by the injured as well as the deceased is not harmful to the prosecution in any manner rather it supports the ocular account. The observation made by learned trial court in that regard is the clear-cut result of misreading of the evidence available on record. 14. The non-production of two material and independent witnesses namely, Wilayat Khan and Saifullah was not fatal to the prosecution in terms of Article 129(g) of the Qanoon-i-Shahadat Order, 1984 because the settled principle of law, which matters in the dispensation of criminal justice is the quality and not quantity of the evidence.

The observation made by learned trial court in that regard is the clear-cut result of misreading of the evidence available on record. 14. The non-production of two material and independent witnesses namely, Wilayat Khan and Saifullah was not fatal to the prosecution in terms of Article 129(g) of the Qanoon-i-Shahadat Order, 1984 because the settled principle of law, which matters in the dispensation of criminal justice is the quality and not quantity of the evidence. The prosecution produced two material and natural witnesses being inmates including an injured girl, who supported each other on all material particulars of the incident coupled with the medical evidence. It is the exclusive prerogative of the prosecution to produce the witnesses of its own choice without any compulsion to produce all the witnesses, who claimed to have seen the occurrence. Furthermore, it has been held in plethora of judgment that the evidence of a single witness can be made basis for conviction even in a murder case, if it is found to be reliable. See case titled “NIAZ-UD-DIN and another Versus THE STATE and another” (2011 SCMR 725), “The statement of Israel (PW.9) the eye-witness of the occurrence is confidence inspiring, which stand substantiated from the Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 8 circumstances and other evidence. There is apt observations appearing in Allah Bakhsh v. Shammi and others (PLD 1980 SC 225) that even in a murder case conviction can be based on the testimony of a single witness, if the Court is satisfied that he is reliable. The reason being that it is the quality of evidence and not the quantity which matters.” 15. Muhammad Aslam, SI (PW-17), upon completion of all the necessary steps taken during investigation, reached at a definite conclusion that the respondents were not involved in this occurrence but interestingly, he did not make any effort to know the whereabouts of the persons, who had butchered whole family of the complainant including their children. The evidence pro and contra, available on record, does not show even a single ground that how he considered all the respondents and other co-accused being not involved in this occurrence.

Muhammad Aslam, SI (PW-17), upon completion of all the necessary steps taken during investigation, reached at a definite conclusion that the respondents were not involved in this occurrence but interestingly, he did not make any effort to know the whereabouts of the persons, who had butchered whole family of the complainant including their children. The evidence pro and contra, available on record, does not show even a single ground that how he considered all the respondents and other co-accused being not involved in this occurrence. He instead, as discussed above, did not reduce into writing the statements of injured witnesses well in time even though he had sought permission from the doctor on the very next evening of the occurrence. Furthermore, he secured 60-empties of rifle 44-bore, 14-empties of rifle 7-MM, 19-empties of Kalashnikov, 14-empties of pistol 30-bore, 05-empties of gun 12-bore and 12-empties of rifle 08-MM but he did not make any effort to recover any of the aforesaid weapons of offence from the respondents. All this shows the high handedness of his biasness and joining hands with the respondents. The opinion of the investigating officer has no binding effect upon the court but it was haphazardly taken into consideration by the learned trial court. The court should have decided a lis and form its opinion as to the genuineness or otherwise of the claim while relying on the evidence/material available on record instead of merely relying on the police opinion especially when the investigating officer played decisive and vital role in destroying the prosecution evidence. 16. It has been contended that the complainant had entangled maximum male members of the opponent party by spreading the net wide upon whole family members, which is sufficient to discard the prosecution evidence as a whole. We regret to disagree with this contention because the respondents trespassed into the house of the complainant were already known to each other being neighbor, the crime report was registered with sufficient promptitude containing the names with parentage of the respondents. In our Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 9 rural setup when any person is murdered, not only the close blood relatives but also the remote relatives keep ready to take the revenge by considering it their family honour and respect.

In our Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 9 rural setup when any person is murdered, not only the close blood relatives but also the remote relatives keep ready to take the revenge by considering it their family honour and respect. In the present scenario, it was pre-planned and premeditated occurrence wherein the close relatives had fully participated, assisted each other and got the advantage of their unlawful assembly on the eve of death anniversary of Muhammad Ayub, brother of principal accused Muhammad Aslam (since dead). The participation of all the relatives was not unusual rather it was quite possible. If it was not so, some of the relatives could have informed the complainant party for the upcoming unfortunate moments. 17. Gathering all these facts and circumstances of the case, we have reached at an irresistible conclusion that the prosecution has successfully proved the charge of murder against the respondents through cogent, convincing, reliable and confidence inspiring evidence. Both the witnesses of ocular account, being inmates including the one (Mst. Sajida Bibi), who had received five fire arm injuries through and through on different parts of her body, were quite natural and straightforward and there was no ambiguity in any manner regarding their presence at the venue of occurrence. They had no occasion to falsely implicate the respondents in this case while letting off the real culprits of the murders of their family including children. The ocular account was believable, confidence inspiring and nothing is available in contrary. We are surprised that how the learned trial court took such a bold step to discard the evidence on flimsy grounds and speculations, which is the clear-cut result of misreading rather non-reading of the evidence in its true perspective. Moreover, the judgment of acquittal is based on the police opinion, which was the sheer example of dishonesty, biasness and incapacity of the investigating officer, who had bulldozed the evidence for the reason as suggested by the defence itself that accused Muhammad Aslam had sold out his land in millions of rupees, shifted his residence as well as family to some unknown place and managed the police prior to this occurrence.

We are surprised that how the learned trial court took such a bold step to discard the evidence on flimsy grounds and speculations, which is the clear-cut result of misreading rather non-reading of the evidence in its true perspective. Moreover, the judgment of acquittal is based on the police opinion, which was the sheer example of dishonesty, biasness and incapacity of the investigating officer, who had bulldozed the evidence for the reason as suggested by the defence itself that accused Muhammad Aslam had sold out his land in millions of rupees, shifted his residence as well as family to some unknown place and managed the police prior to this occurrence. Another aspect, which is important to divert our intention in this regard as highlighted by the defence itself by putting certain questions to injured Mst. Sajida Bibi, is that she opened the door when it was knocked by some police officials (the unknown accused were in police uniforms), she replied in Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 10 affirmative. It was the sole reason to open the door by the lady otherwise in such background of blood-feud enmity; she would have not opened it on the request or alarm of their opponent. The motive is well established rather admitted by the defence in so many words during cross-examination. Though it was a double-edged weapon either to falsely implicate the accused party or being the sole reason behind the occurrence yet, in present scenario, there is no doubt in our mind that all the family members of the complainant party were butchered by the respondents on account of their previous enmity in order to take the revenge. No recovery of weapon of offence was effected from the respondents due to biasness and high handedness of the investigating officer. Anyhow, in the presence of strong ocular account coupled with the medical evidence as well as motive, the existing facts and circumstances of the case favours the prosecution and proved that none else but the respondents had kill all the deceased and caused fire arm injuries to others. We have reasons to differ with the opinion arrived at by the learned trial court qua the acquittal of the respondents. We are inclined to convert the judgment of acquittal into one of conviction of the respondents. 18. We have deep concern that it was the enmity between two individuals, which does not constitute the offence of ‘terrorism’.

We are inclined to convert the judgment of acquittal into one of conviction of the respondents. 18. We have deep concern that it was the enmity between two individuals, which does not constitute the offence of ‘terrorism’. Though seven persons lost their lives and three others had received injuries, all belonging to the same family yet the fact remains that the said act was the result of specific motive and personal vendetta and nothing else, which does not fall within the categories specified under subsection of Section 6 of the Act ibid. The acquittal of the respondents in Section 7 of the Act ibid shall remain intact. 19. So far as culpability of the respondents regarding offences falling under the provisions of Section 302(b), 324, 449, 148 & 149 PPC is concerned, they gathered at one place with their common object, assaulted upon the complainant party with pre-planning and premeditation, while trespassing into their house, murdered seven innocent inmates and caused fire arm injuries to four other inmates. We are fully convinced that the prosecution has successfully proved their case against the respondents beyond shadow of reasonable doubt and hold them guilty of the aforesaid offences. So far as the quantum of sentence is concerned, we have observed Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 11 that no injury was specifically attributed to any of the respondents and it remained uncertain throughout the trial that whose fire shot hit to whom of the injured/deceased. It has also been observed that the respondents were acquitted by the learned trial court way back in the year, 2008 and at this stage, after more than ten years, the capital sentence is not warranted. 20. The epitome of above discussion is that the appeal in hand is hereby dismissed to the extent of respondents Mst. Zahida Bibi, Mst. Nusrat Bibi, Muhammad Azam and Asjad Mehmood and their acquittal is hereby maintained. 21.

Zahida Bibi, Mst. Nusrat Bibi, Muhammad Azam and Asjad Mehmood and their acquittal is hereby maintained. 21. This appeal however, is hereby allowed and impugned judgment dated 22.05.2008 is set aside to the extent of respondents Muhammad Akbar, Zafar Iqbal son of Bahadar, Sarfraz alias Ibrar, Sajid Iqbal, Muhammad Malik, Zafar Iqbal son of Muhammad Shafi, Amjad Iqbal, Bashir Ahmed, Mehndi Khan, Zulfiqar and Muhammad Anar and they are convicted and sentenced as under:- Under Section 302(b)/149 PPC Imprisonment for life on seven counts each and to pay the compensation of Rs.100,000/- on seven counts each to the legal heirs of each deceased under Section 544-A Cr.P.C., which shall be recoverable as arrears of land revenue and in default thereof, to further undergo simple imprisonment for six months. Under Section 324/149 PPC Rigorous imprisonment for five years in three counts each and also to pay the fine of Rs.20,000/- each and in default thereof, to further undergo simple imprisonment for three months each. Under Section 449/149 PPC Rigorous imprisonment for three years each and also to pay the fine of Rs.10,000/- each and in default thereof, to further undergo simple imprisonment for two months each. Under Section 148 PPC Rigorous imprisonment for three years each with the fine of Rs.10,000/- each and in default thereof, to further undergo simple imprisonment for two months each. Crl. Appeal No.601 of 2008 12 The respondents are given benefit of Section 382-B Cr.P.C. All the aforesaid sentences shall run concurrently. The respondents shall be taken into custody and be lodged in a prison so as to serve their remaining sentences. (Muhammad Qasim Khan) (Asjad Javaid Ghural) Judge Judge Announced in open Court on 16.05.2019.

All the aforesaid sentences shall run concurrently. The respondents shall be taken into custody and be lodged in a prison so as to serve their remaining sentences. (Muhammad Qasim Khan) (Asjad Javaid Ghural) Judge Judge Announced in open Court on 16.05.2019. Judge Judge Asif*
